title: GrokCoder chat session document
description: Schema for the documents stored in the Couchbase `report` collection by the GrokCoder VS Code extension. One document represents one chat session. The session is captured as an ordered `chatHistory` array of turns; each turn carries a single `message` plus optional pinned `contextItems`, the resolved `toolCallStates` for any tools the assistant asked to call on that turn, and the raw `promptLogs` (verbatim prompt + completion) that were sent to / received from the model. See sample/chat_sample.json for a representative document.

chatHistory.description: Ordered list of conversational turns, oldest first.

repo.description: Optional git workspace metadata captured by the extension at the time of the session. Used by the auto-triage pipeline so the proposed fix targets the branch the bug was actually observed on (may diverge from the GitHub default branch). All sub-fields are best-effort; omit the whole block if the workspace is not a git checkout.

RepoContext.description: Git workspace snapshot for the session.

name.description: Logical repo name (typically `owner/repo` or workspace folder).

branch.description: Active branch at the time the bug was observed. Falls back to the default branch only if the workspace was in a detached-HEAD state.

commit.description: Full or short HEAD commit SHA.

dirty.description: True if the working tree had uncommitted changes when the session ran.

contextItems.description: Files / snippets the user pinned into the prompt for this turn. Typically only present on the first user turn.

promptLogs.description: Raw prompt + completion pair(s) exchanged with the LLM on this turn. Usually only set on assistant turns.

toolCallStates.description: Resolved execution state for each tool call the assistant requested on this turn (params, output, error, status). One entry per `message.toolCalls[]` item.

content.description: For `user`/`tool` messages this is typically a plain string. For `assistant`/`user` messages it may be an array of typed content parts (most commonly `{ text, type: 'text' }`). It is an empty string on assistant turns that only emit tool calls.

toolCallId.description: Set on `role='tool'` messages — points back to the `ToolCall.id` (and `ToolCallState.toolCallId`) this response is for.

toolCalls.description: Set on `role='assistant'` messages when the model emitted one or more function-style tool calls.

itemId.description: Stable UUID for this context item.

providerTitle.description: Which context provider produced the item.

content.description: Inlined contents, often a fenced ```file code block.

prompt.description: Verbatim text sent to the model (system + user + assistant + tool turns concatenated using <system>, <user>, <assistant>, <tool> tags).

completion.description: Verbatim assistant completion returned by the model.

id.description: Tool-call ID. Referenced from the matching `ToolCallState.toolCallId` and from the `Message.toolCallId` on the following tool-result message.

arguments.description: JSON-encoded arguments string exactly as emitted by the model. Parse with JSON.parse; the decoded form is also available on `ToolCallState.parsedArgs`.

status.description: Final execution status. `errored` implies a non-empty `error` object.

output.description: Tool result(s). Present on `status='done'`, and often also on `status='errored'` (carrying the failure message).

error.description: Present (and non-empty) when `status='errored'`. The dashboard surfaces these in the Tool Errors chart and table.

displayTitle.description: Human-readable title shown in the UI, e.g. 'Agent router refine_request'.

parameters.description: JSON Schema (typically draft-07) for the tool's parameters.

group.description: Logical grouping for the tool, e.g. 'Agent router', 'Built-In'.

uri.description: MCP URI when the tool is exposed over Model Context Protocol.
